title: Startup raises $85M for AI in logistics, predicts industry shift - LinkedIn
full_text:
Startup raises $85M for workflow-native AI in logistics
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
David Hauser
9mo
Report this post
$110M raised in 5 months.
A one-year-old startup just raised $85M in a Series A round led by Redpoint Ventures, with participation from 8VC, Shopify Ventures, Autotech Ventures, and others.
That tells us investors believe:
– Workflow-native AI (trained on logistics data + processes) creates defensible moats.
– Adoption curves are faster when AI feels like a teammate, not another SaaS login.
– The TAM is huge. Logistics is a trillion-dollar industry with margin pressure and labor bottlenecks.
Logistics is just one example.
Similar vertical plays are emerging in:
Legal → Contract review agents.
Finance → AI underwriters, wealth management agents.
Construction → AI safety monitors and project schedulers.
Healthcare → Ambient clinical documentation, revenue cycle AI.
Augment’s raise is a datapoint in the larger trend:
📌 Capital is moving from broad AI platforms into applied, industry-specific AI.
What AI are you building?
_______________
P.S. My newsletter (Join 20K+ subscribers): https://lnkd.in/dNVbGxXv
My reading list: Reading List | https://lnkd.in/dS8XJ9_W

Monwabisi Mclean
9mo
Report this comment
David Hauser ,
Good Evening Mr Hauser,
My name is Benjamin Monwabisi McLean. I thought let me share my proposal with you since i need Funding for Ready-Mix Trucks.
I'm entrepreneur doing business in the Construction Sector. I received a Contract from AfriSam South Africa who want to rope me in under their Ready-mix/Concrete Division.
I need to bring on board 6 Trucks namely 3 trucks in Cape Town and 3 in Kzn respectively. I need funding to the tune of R 9 million Rands to source 6 trucks or R1,500 000 for Working Capital.
The contract is for period of 5 years and they will pay me R997,000 per month. I've also prepared the financial projections as well for your perusal which indicate on how I anticipate the project to unfold.
Combined projects on the project value being 28 Billion Rands, with the Project in KZN being 8 Billion rands and the client being SANRAL and the Project in Cape Town is being Funded by PIC and its the Redevelopment of the V& A Waterfront. The Project value is 28 Billion Rands.
If you have any questions you can reach me on +27736271618.
I'll await on your prompt response on the way forward.
Kind Regards,
Mr McLean

Startup Verge

Report this post
Accordance , founded by Stanford dropouts David Yue and Finsam Samson , raised $13 million in seed and pre-seed funding, led by Khosla Ventures and General Catalyst , to launch its sophisticated multi-agent AI platform for tax and accounting professionals. By simulating expert workflows, Accordance aims to empower junior staff with on-demand insights while freeing senior experts to focus on strategic work amid industry talent shortages.
Built in partnership with OpenAI , the platform navigates complex global tax codes and compliance, significantly reducing time spent on regulatory research and audit preparations. Over 50 CPA firms participated in beta testing, highlighting the solution’s real-world readiness and interoperability with tools like Microsoft 365.
As competition in AI accounting intensifies, Accordance stands out for its domain expertise and human-in-the-loop validation, promising to advance professional service automation while addressing accuracy and compliance challenges.
#StartupNews #AI #AccountingTech #Fintech #FundingUpdate #StartupNews #Founders #Entrepreneur #StartupIndia #StartupVerge #Startup

8mo
Report this post
Ramp acquires Jolt AI (acquired by Ramp) team to accelerate engineering productivity and AI development
New York–based #fintech #Ramp , valued at $22.5 billion with $1 billion in annualized revenue in 2025, has acquired the three-person team of Jolt AI to strengthen its AI engineering capabilities. Founded in 2022 by Yev Spektor , Jolt AI had raised nearly $2.5 million from Alumni Ventures , 8-Bit Capital , and Engineering Capital , pivoting in 2024 from load-testing tools to AI-powered coding assistants for production-scale software.
Ramp’s co-founder and CTO Karim Atiyeh said the acquisition is aimed at making Ramp’s 1,200 engineers “radically more productive,” enhancing internal AI devtools and developing agentic products for its 45,000+ customers, including Shopify, Stripe, Notion, and ZipRecruiter. The Jolt team—Spektor, Jon Reynolds , and Carlos Kelly —will join Ramp’s engineering platform unit, focusing on applied AI, infrastructure, and customer-facing financial workflows.
Ramp has been actively expanding through AI-driven acquisitions, previously buying Cohere in 2023 to improve AI customer support and Venue in 2024 to automate procurement. Since its founding in 2019 by Eric Glyman and Karim Atiyeh, Ramp has raised $1.9 billion from investors including Iconiq Capital, Founders Fund, Khosla Ventures, Sequoia Capital, Stripe, and T. Rowe Price.
The article on Crunchbase in the first comment.

I was utterly intrigued by the notion of building an entire SaaS company in just 48 hours, 𝙒𝙄𝙏𝙃𝙊𝙐𝙏𝗻𝙒𝙍𝙄𝙏𝙄𝙉𝙂𝗻𝘾𝙊𝘿𝙀𝗻𝘽𝙔𝗻𝙃𝘼𝙉𝘿! 🤯 This isn't just a challenge; it's a profound demonstration of how modern tools are reshaping our ability to 𝗯𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗲𝘃𝗲 𝗶𝗻 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗮𝘁 𝘂𝗻𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗲𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗲𝗱𝘀.
For me, a key insight here is the power of 𝘥𝘳𝗮𝘄𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗻𝗼-𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝘄-𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝘀. These tools aren't just for MVPs; they enable rapid iteration and testing of ideas, which is crucial for identifying product-market fit early. This accelerated feedback loop is invaluable for any startup aiming to scale efficiently. 🚀
Another crucial takeaway is the strategic shift towards leveraging AI and automation. Building without 'hand-coding' implies a significant reliance on intelligent systems to handle development, deployment, and even operational tasks. This frees up human capital to focus on strategic growth, customer acquisition, and market analysis – core elements of a scalable SaaS business. 📈
This trend clearly shows that the traditional barriers to entry for launching and scaling a tech venture are significantly lower than ever before. It's an exciting time to be an entrepreneur.
What role do you see AI and no-code tools playing in the future of scaling SaaS businesses?
#SaaSScaling #NoCode #LowCode #AIinBusiness #StartupGrowth

OpenAI just acquired Roi, a personalized investing chatbot startup. Here's what nobody's talking about:
→ Acquired Roi (announced Oct 4, 2025). Roi built a personalized AI investing chatbot that gives tailored financial advice.
→ No disclosed price, no clarity on fiduciary duty, and real questions around SEC and CFPB oversight.
→ Great for small businesses seeking affordable advice. Risky for privacy and model accuracy when financial data feeds an LLM.
Not gonna lie, this surprised me. We tested a similar finance bot last quarter and data hygiene blew up fast.
Game changer or regulatory headache? What's your take on OpenAI moving into consumer finance? 👇
#OpenAI #Roi #FinTech #AIRegulation

## clean_text

Startup raises $85M for workflow-native AI in logistics
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
David Hauser
9mo
Report this post
$110M raised in 5 months.
A one-year-old startup just raised $85M in a Series A round led by Redpoint Ventures, with participation from 8VC, Shopify Ventures, Autotech Ventures, and others.
That tells us investors believe:
– Workflow-native AI (trained on logistics data + processes) creates defensible moats.
– Adoption curves are faster when AI feels like a teammate, not another SaaS login.
– The TAM is huge. Logistics is a trillion-dollar industry with margin pressure and labor bottlenecks.
Logistics is just one example.
Similar vertical plays are emerging in:
Legal → Contract review agents.
Finance → AI underwriters, wealth management agents.
Construction → AI safety monitors and project schedulers.
Healthcare → Ambient clinical documentation, revenue cycle AI.
Augment’s raise is a datapoint in the larger trend:
📌 Capital is moving from broad AI platforms into applied, industry-specific AI.
What AI are you building?
_______________
P.S. My newsletter (Join 20K+ subscribers): https://lnkd.in/dNVbGxXv

David Hauser ,
Good Evening Mr Hauser,
My name is Benjamin Monwabisi McLean. I thought let me share my proposal with you since i need Funding for Ready-Mix Trucks.
I'm entrepreneur doing business in the Construction Sector. I received a Contract from AfriSam South Africa who want to rope me in under their Ready-mix/Concrete Division.
I need to bring on board 6 Trucks namely 3 trucks in Cape Town and 3 in Kzn respectively. I need funding to the tune of R 9 million Rands to source 6 trucks or R1,500 000 for Working Capital.
The contract is for period of 5 years and they will pay me R997,000 per month. I've also prepared the financial projections as well for your perusal which indicate on how I anticipate the project to unfold.
Combined projects on the project value being 28 Billion Rands, with the Project in KZN being 8 Billion rands and the client being SANRAL and the Project in Cape Town is being Funded by PIC and its the Redevelopment of the V& A Waterfront. The Project value is 28 Billion Rands.
If you have any questions you can reach me on +27736271618.
I'll await on your prompt response on the way forward.
Kind Regards,
Mr McLean

Accordance , founded by Stanford dropouts David Yue and Finsam Samson , raised $13 million in seed and pre-seed funding, led by Khosla Ventures and General Catalyst , to launch its sophisticated multi-agent AI platform for tax and accounting professionals. By simulating expert workflows, Accordance aims to empower junior staff with on-demand insights while freeing senior experts to focus on strategic work amid industry talent shortages.
Built in partnership with OpenAI , the platform navigates complex global tax codes and compliance, significantly reducing time spent on regulatory research and audit preparations. Over 50 CPA firms participated in beta testing, highlighting the solution’s real-world readiness and interoperability with tools like Microsoft 365.
As competition in AI accounting intensifies, Accordance stands out for its domain expertise and human-in-the-loop validation, promising to advance professional service automation while addressing accuracy and compliance challenges.

Ramp acquires Jolt AI (acquired by Ramp) team to accelerate engineering productivity and AI development
New York–based #fintech #Ramp , valued at $22.5 billion with $1 billion in annualized revenue in 2025, has acquired the three-person team of Jolt AI to strengthen its AI engineering capabilities. Founded in 2022 by Yev Spektor , Jolt AI had raised nearly $2.5 million from Alumni Ventures , 8-Bit Capital , and Engineering Capital , pivoting in 2024 from load-testing tools to AI-powered coding assistants for production-scale software.
Ramp’s co-founder and CTO Karim Atiyeh said the acquisition is aimed at making Ramp’s 1,200 engineers “radically more productive,” enhancing internal AI devtools and developing agentic products for its 45,000+ customers, including Shopify, Stripe, Notion, and ZipRecruiter. The Jolt team—Spektor, Jon Reynolds , and Carlos Kelly —will join Ramp’s engineering platform unit, focusing on applied AI, infrastructure, and customer-facing financial workflows.
Ramp has been actively expanding through AI-driven acquisitions, previously buying Cohere in 2023 to improve AI customer support and Venue in 2024 to automate procurement. Since its founding in 2019 by Eric Glyman and Karim Atiyeh, Ramp has raised $1.9 billion from investors including Iconiq Capital, Founders Fund, Khosla Ventures, Sequoia Capital, Stripe, and T. Rowe Price.
The article on Crunchbase in the first comment.

I was utterly intrigued by the notion of building an entire SaaS company in just 48 hours, 𝙒𝙄𝙏𝙃𝙊𝙐𝙏𝗻𝙒𝙍𝙄𝙏𝙄𝙉𝙂𝗻𝘾𝙊𝘿𝙀𝗻𝘽𝙔𝗻𝙃𝘼𝙉𝘿! 🤯 This isn't just a challenge; it's a profound demonstration of how modern tools are reshaping our ability to 𝗯𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗲𝘃𝗲 𝗶𝗻 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗮𝘁 𝘂𝗻𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗲𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗲𝗱𝘀.
For me, a key insight here is the power of 𝘥𝘳𝗮𝘄𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗻𝗼-𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝘄-𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝘀. These tools aren't just for MVPs; they enable rapid iteration and testing of ideas, which is crucial for identifying product-market fit early. This accelerated feedback loop is invaluable for any startup aiming to scale efficiently. 🚀
Another crucial takeaway is the strategic shift towards leveraging AI and automation. Building without 'hand-coding' implies a significant reliance on intelligent systems to handle development, deployment, and even operational tasks. This frees up human capital to focus on strategic growth, customer acquisition, and market analysis – core elements of a scalable SaaS business. 📈
This trend clearly shows that the traditional barriers to entry for launching and scaling a tech venture are significantly lower than ever before. It's an exciting time to be an entrepreneur.
What role do you see AI and no-code tools playing in the future of scaling SaaS businesses?
#SaaSScaling #NoCode #LowCode #AIinBusiness #StartupGrowth

OpenAI just acquired Roi, a personalized investing chatbot startup. Here's what nobody's talking about:
→ Acquired Roi (announced Oct 4, 2025). Roi built a personalized AI investing chatbot that gives tailored financial advice.
→ No disclosed price, no clarity on fiduciary duty, and real questions around SEC and CFPB oversight.
→ Great for small businesses seeking affordable advice. Risky for privacy and model accuracy when financial data feeds an LLM.
Not gonna lie, this surprised me. We tested a similar finance bot last quarter and data hygiene blew up fast.
Game changer or regulatory headache? What's your take on OpenAI moving into consumer finance? 👇
#OpenAI #Roi #FinTech #AIRegulation

## full_text

Startup raises $85M for workflow-native AI in logistics
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
David Hauser
9mo
Report this post
$110M raised in 5 months.
A one-year-old startup just raised $85M in a Series A round led by Redpoint Ventures, with participation from 8VC, Shopify Ventures, Autotech Ventures, and others.
That tells us investors believe:
– Workflow-native AI (trained on logistics data + processes) creates defensible moats.
– Adoption curves are faster when AI feels like a teammate, not another SaaS login.
– The TAM is huge. Logistics is a trillion-dollar industry with margin pressure and labor bottlenecks.
Logistics is just one example.
Similar vertical plays are emerging in:
Legal → Contract review agents.
Finance → AI underwriters, wealth management agents.
Construction → AI safety monitors and project schedulers.
Healthcare → Ambient clinical documentation, revenue cycle AI.
Augment’s raise is a datapoint in the larger trend:
📌 Capital is moving from broad AI platforms into applied, industry-specific AI.
What AI are you building?
_______________

For me, a key insight here is the power of 𝘥𝘳𝗮𝘄𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗻𝗼-𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗹𝗼𝘄-𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝘀. These tools aren't just for MVPs; they enable rapid iteration and testing of ideas, which is crucial for identifying product-market fit early. This accelerated feedback loop is invaluable for any startup aiming to scale efficiently. 🚀
Another crucial takeaway is the strategic shift towards leveraging AI and automation. Building without 'hand-coding' implies a significant reliance on intelligent systems to handle development, deployment, and even operational tasks. This frees up human capital to focus on strategic growth, customer acquisition, and market analysis – core elements of a scalable SaaS business. 📈
This trend clearly shows that the traditional barriers to entry for launching and scaling a tech venture are significantly lower than ever before. It's an exciting time to be an entrepreneur.
What role do you see AI and no-code tools playing in the future of scaling SaaS businesses?

OpenAI just acquired Roi, a personalized investing chatbot startup. Here's what nobody's talking about:
→ Acquired Roi (announced Oct 4, 2025). Roi built a personalized AI investing chatbot that gives tailored financial advice.
→ No disclosed price, no clarity on fiduciary duty, and real questions around SEC and CFPB oversight.
→ Great for small businesses seeking affordable advice. Risky for privacy and model accuracy when financial data feeds an LLM.
Not gonna lie, this surprised me. We tested a similar finance bot last quarter and data hygiene blew up fast.
Game changer or regulatory headache? What's your take on OpenAI moving into consumer finance? 👇
